ip_outcome_note: Vision flagged 'Son Gohan (child) or Son Goku (child)' at high confidence. The card text confirms this is NOT a character card at all — BT3-027 is a Counter card with skill '[Counter: Attack] Negate the attack. Then, choose up to 1 of your red Saiyan Leader Cards. It gains +5000 power for the duration of the turn.' It has no character association in the official card database — it is a generic support card. The child in orange gi screaming in a Super Saiyan power-up is illustrative art representing the concept of Saiyan awakening, not depicting any specific named character. Per anti-confab rules: no oracle text names a character, no flavor text names a character, the card is categorized as a Counter (not a Battle card), and no Wizards/Bandai article links this specific art to a named character. Refute both candidates.

trivia_bullets.text: Unending Awakening (BT3-027) is a red Common Counter card from Cross Worlds (DBS-B03, March 2018). Unlike Battle cards, it depicts no specific named character — it illustrates the thematic concept of a Saiyan's Super Saiyan awakening without tying the art to a single named individual [Source: WebSearch snippets from coretcg.com, trollandtoad.com, and dbzexchange.com BT3-027 listings].
sources: WebSearch snippets: coretcg.com, trollandtoad.com, dbzexchange.com BT3-027 listings

trivia_bullets.text: BT3-027's skill: '[Counter: Attack] Negate the attack. Then, choose up to 1 of your red Saiyan Leader Cards. It gains +5000 power for the duration of the turn.' It is a universal red Saiyan support counter — works with any red Saiyan Leader, not locked to a specific character. This makes it a flexible generic inclusion in any red Saiyan deck [Source: WebSearch snippets from coretcg.com and dbzexchange.com BT3-027 listings].
sources: WebSearch snippets: coretcg.com, dbzexchange.com BT3-027 listings

trivia_bullets.text: The Super Saiyan transformation depicted — screaming child, golden aura erupting — is one of Dragon Ball's most iconic visual motifs. Its most famous in-story occurrence is young Gohan's first Super Saiyan 2 awakening against Cell (Dragon Ball Z, 1993); Goku's first Super Saiyan transformation in a child-adjacent context occurs in Dragon Ball GT. The art evokes the archetype, not either specific moment, making the figure intentionally ambiguous [Source: Dragon Ball Wiki: Super Saiyan; CBR: Future Trunks timeline].
